Previous

INTRODUCTION
Welcome to the official website of 
							2026 7th International Conference on Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2026)! 
							NLPAI 2026 will be held in Urumqi, China during 
							July 10-13, 2026. NLPAI 2026 aims 
							to provide a forum for researchers, practitioners, 
							and professionals from the industry, academia and 
							government to discourse on research and development, 
							professionals practice in the field of Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence.
We welcome 
							researchers, engineers, students, industry, 
							investors and people from all walks of life 
							interested in Natural Language Processing and 
							Artificial Intelligence to attend the conference and 
							share your experiences and lessons with other 
							enthusiasts, and develop opportunities for 
							cooperation
.
PAPER PUBLICATION
Submitted papers will be peer reviewed by Technical 
							Committee members or international reviewers. All 
							accepted papers will be published in
International 
							Conference Proceedings by ACM
(ISBN:
							979-8-4007-2497-8)
, which will be archived in
ACM Digital 
							Library
, indexed by
Ei Compendex
and
Scopus
, and 
							submitted to be reviewed by Thomson Reuters 
							Conference Proceedings Citation Index (ISI Web of 
							Science).
To be published in the Conference 
							Proceedings, the author of an accepted paper is 
							required to register for the conference and present 
							his/her paper.
NEWS
All accepted papers will be published in International Conference Proceedings by ACM(ISBN: 979-8-4007-2497-8)

=== PAGE: https://www.nlpai.org/visa.html ===
Link: VISA TO CHINA
Title: NLPAI 2026|Urumiqi,China
NLPAI 2026|Urumiqi,China
Skip to content
2026 7th International 
								Conference on
Natural Language Processing 
								and Artificial Intelligence (NLPAI 2026)
July 10-13, 2026, 
								Urumqi, China
VISA TO CHINA
Conference participants may need 
							a Chinese visa to enter China. Specifically, those 
							who are not Chinese citizens, except passport 
							holders from Singapore, Brunei, and Japan, need a 
							Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a visa at 
							the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region in 
							which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
							will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
							possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
							Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a 
							Tourist ("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT 
							required for obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary Materials to Apply for Chinese 
							Visa
脗禄A passport which is valid for at 
							least six months following the date of submission, 
							with an entire blank page available for the visa;
脗禄One Visa Application Form of the Peoples Republic 
							of China, which is filled clearly with true, 
							authentic and complete information and signed by the 
							applicant;
脗禄One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded 
							passport photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or 
							digital photo printed on the ordinary paper shall 
							not be accepted);
脗禄Other related application materials according to 
							different purposes for your trip to China;
脗禄Other materials related to the visa application, 
							deemed as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local 
							Chinese Embassy in your country or visit the 
							website:
http://www.visaforchina.org/
.
Alternatively, participants may ask their travel 
							agents to arrange their travel to China and obtain a 
							tourist visa irrespective of whether or not they 
							have registered with the Conference Secretariat. If 
							participants have any difficulties in obtaining 
							their visa, they should contact the Conference 
							Secretary.
Invitation Letter
Please note NLPAI 2026 is not 
							authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond 
							providing the Notification of Acceptance Letter and 
							Invitation Letter issued by the organizing 
							committee.
* Invitation letter will only be issued upon request 
							after successful registration.
Note:
Should your application be denied, NLPAI cannot 
							change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign 
							Affairs, nor will NLPAI engage in discussion or 
							correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on 
							behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot 
							be refunded when the VISA application of individual 
							being denied.

							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2026) is the premier forum for the 
							presentation of new advances and research results in 
							the fields of theoretical, experimental, and applied 
							Natural Language Processing and Artificial

							leading researchers, engineers and scientists in the 
							domain of interest from around the world. Topics of 
							interest for submission include, but are not limited

S
Assoc. Prof. Samir Rustamov
ADA University, Azerbaijan
I got my Ph.D. degree in Computer Science from 
							Institute of Cybernetics and I was post-doctoral 
							researcher at Georgia Institute of Technology, USA. 
							I am an Associate Professor in School of IT and 
							Engineering, ADA University. My interests are 
							natural language processing and machine learning. I 
							am particularly interested in how to use language 
							understanding to generalize and learn more 
							efficiently. I am also head of AI laboratory at 
							MegaSec Company. I have worked on a range of 
							applications including Speech Recogition, Speech 
							Synthesis, Dialogue System, Deep Learning, Sentiment 
							Analysis and developed AI -based products for 
							different companies. I help organisations to build

Speech Title: "From Lab to

SPEAKER
S
Prof. Xiwen Zhang
Beijing Language and Culture University, China
Xiwen Zhang is currently a Professor of Digital 
							Media Department, School of Information Science in 
							the Beijing Language and Culture University. He 
							worked as an associated professor from 2002 to 2007 
							at the Human-computer interaction Laboratory, 
							Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences. 
							From 2005 to 2006 he was a Post doctor advised by 
							Prof. Michael R. Lyu in the Department of Computer 
							Science and Engineering, the Chinese University of 
							Hong Kong. From February to April in 2001 he was a 
							Research Assistant by Dr. KeZhang Chen in the 
							Department of Mechanical Engineering, the University 
							of Hong Kong. From 2000 to 2002 he was a Post doctor 
							advised by Prof. ShiJie Cai in the Computer Science 
							and Technology department, Nanjing University. Prof. 
							Zhang's research interests include pattern 
							recognition, computer vision, and human-computer 
							interaction, as well as their applications in 
							digital image, digital video, and digital ink. Prof. 
							Zhang has published over 60 refereed journal and

Dr. Dmitry Ustalov
Research at Toloka AI, Russia
Dr. Dmitry Ustalov is the Head of Research at 
							Toloka, a global data labeling platform. He is 
							responsible for enabling the state-of-the-art 
							methods for quality control in Toloka and spreading 
							the innovations made by the Toloka Research team. 
							His research interests focus on Crowdsourcing and 
							Natural Language Processing; his research is 
							published in the leading scientific venues, such as 
							NeurIPS, COLI, ACL, EACL, and EMNLP. Dmitry has been 
							co-organizing Crowd Science Workshop at NeurIPS and 
							VLDB since 2020, and TextGraphs workshop at ACL 
							since 2018. He co-authored and presented 
							crowdsourcing tutorials at NAACL-HLT '21, WWW '21, 
							SIGMOD/PODS '20, and WSDM '20.

July 10-13, 2026,

KEYNOTE 
			SPEAKER
of NLPAI 2025
Prof.
Yue Zhang
Westlake University
, China
Yue Zhang is a tenured Professor 
							at Westlake University. His research interests 
							include NLP and its underlying machine learning 
							algorithms. His major contributions to the field 
							include psycholinguistically motivated machine 
							learning algorithm, learning-guided beam search for 
							structured prediction, pioneering neural NLP models 
							including graph LSTM, and OOD generalization for 
							NLP. He authored the Cambridge University Press book 
							``Natural Language Processing -- a Machine Learning 
							Perspective''. He is the PC co-chair for CCL 2020 
							and EMNLP 2022, and action editor for Transactios 
							for ACL. He also served as associate editor for 
							IEEE/ACM Transactions of Audio Speech and Language 
							Processing (TASLP), ACM Transactions on Asian and 
							Low-Resource Languages (TALLIP), IEEE Transactions 
							on Big Data (TBD) and Computer, Speech and Language 
							(CSL). He won the best paper awards of IALP 2017 and 
							COLING 2018, best paper honorable mention of SemEval 
							2020, and best paper nomination for ACL 2018 and ACL 
							2023.
Speech Title: "AI Scientist"
Abstract: The 
							rise of LLMs allows systems to perform complex 
							reasoning, which leads to the exploration of AI 
							scientists. Ideally, AI scientists should be able to 
							generate new ideas, rea relevant literature, design 
							the implementation, conduct experiments, and write 
							academic reports. Existing research has achieved 
							initial milestones on some of the functionalities. 
							In this talk, I will share recent work on using AI 
							to facilitate research activities, including 
							literature survey, recommendation, and knowledge 
							organization. Then I will discuss a system that 
							automatically conducts research given a direction, 
							working towards a finished article, and revising its 
							work according to reviewer comments.

2026 7th International 
								Conference on
Natural Language Processing 
								and Artificial Intelligence (NLPAI 2026)
July 10-13, 2026, 
								Urumqi, China
Previous
Next
INTRODUCTION
Welcome to the official website of 
							2026 7th International Conference on Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2026)! 
							NLPAI 2026 will be held in Urumqi, China during 
							July 10-13, 2026. NLPAI 2026 aims 
							to provide a forum for researchers, practitioners, 
							and professionals from the industry, academia and 
							government to discourse on research and development, 
							professionals practice in the field of Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence.
We welcome 
							researchers, engineers, students, industry, 
							investors and people from all walks of life 
							interested in Natural Language Processing and 
							Artificial Intelligence to attend the conference and 
							share your experiences and lessons with other 
							enthusiasts, and develop opportunities for 
							cooperation
.
PAPER PUBLICATION
Submitted papers will be peer reviewed by Technical 
							Committee members or international reviewers. All 
							accepted papers will be published in
International 
							Conference Proceedings by ACM
(ISBN:
							979-8-4007-2497-8)
, which will be archived in
ACM Digital 
							Library
, indexed by
Ei Compendex
and
Scopus
, and 
							submitted to be reviewed by Thomson Reuters 
							Conference Proceedings Citation Index (ISI Web of 
							Science).
To be published in the Conference 
							Proceedings, the author of an accepted paper is 
							required to register for the conference and present 
							his/her paper.
NEWS
All accepted papers will be published in International Conference Proceedings by ACM(ISBN: 979-8-4007-2497-8)

July 10-13, 2026,

KEYNOTE 
			SPEAKER
of NLPAI 2025
Prof.
Yue Zhang
Westlake University
, China
Yue Zhang is a tenured Professor 
							at Westlake University. His research interests 
							include NLP and its underlying machine learning 
							algorithms. His major contributions to the field 
							include psycholinguistically motivated machine 
							learning algorithm, learning-guided beam search for 
							structured prediction, pioneering neural NLP models 
							including graph LSTM, and OOD generalization for 
							NLP. He authored the Cambridge University Press book 
							``Natural Language Processing -- a Machine Learning 
							Perspective''. He is the PC co-chair for CCL 2020 
							and EMNLP 2022, and action editor for Transactios 
							for ACL. He also served as associate editor for 
							IEEE/ACM Transactions of Audio Speech and Language 
							Processing (TASLP), ACM Transactions on Asian and 
							Low-Resource Languages (TALLIP), IEEE Transactions 
							on Big Data (TBD) and Computer, Speech and Language 
							(CSL). He won the best paper awards of IALP 2017 and 
							COLING 2018, best paper honorable mention of SemEval 
							2020, and best paper nomination for ACL 2018 and ACL 
							2023.
Speech Title: "AI Scientist"
Abstract: The 
							rise of LLMs allows systems to perform complex 
							reasoning, which leads to the exploration of AI 
							scientists. Ideally, AI scientists should be able to 
							generate new ideas, rea relevant literature, design 
							the implementation, conduct experiments, and write 
							academic reports. Existing research has achieved 
							initial milestones on some of the functionalities. 
							In this talk, I will share recent work on using AI 
							to facilitate research activities, including 
							literature survey, recommendation, and knowledge 
							organization. Then I will discuss a system that 
							automatically conducts research given a direction, 
							working towards a finished article, and revising its 
							work according to reviewer comments.

S
Assoc. Prof. Samir Rustamov
ADA University, Azerbaijan
I got my Ph.D. degree in Computer Science from 
							Institute of Cybernetics and I was post-doctoral 
							researcher at Georgia Institute of Technology, USA. 
							I am an Associate Professor in School of IT and 
							Engineering, ADA University. My interests are 
							natural language processing and machine learning. I 
							am particularly interested in how to use language 
							understanding to generalize and learn more 
							efficiently. I am also head of AI laboratory at 
							MegaSec Company. I have worked on a range of 
							applications including Speech Recogition, Speech 
							Synthesis, Dialogue System, Deep Learning, Sentiment 
							Analysis and developed AI -based products for 
							different companies. I help organisations to build 
							AI and machine learning into their products and 
							services, with a particular focus on voice and 
							language technology.
Speech Title: "From Lab to 
							Reality: Building Practical Speech Recognition 
							Systems"
PREVIOUS
SPEAKER
S
Prof. Xiwen Zhang
Beijing Language and Culture University, China
Xiwen Zhang is currently a Professor of Digital 
							Media Department, School of Information Science in 
							the Beijing Language and Culture University. He 
							worked as an associated professor from 2002 to 2007 
							at the Human-computer interaction Laboratory, 
							Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences. 
							From 2005 to 2006 he was a Post doctor advised by 
							Prof. Michael R. Lyu in the Department of Computer 
							Science and Engineering, the Chinese University of 
							Hong Kong. From February to April in 2001 he was a 
							Research Assistant by Dr. KeZhang Chen in the 
							Department of Mechanical Engineering, the University 
							of Hong Kong. From 2000 to 2002 he was a Post doctor 
							advised by Prof. ShiJie Cai in the Computer Science 
							and Technology department, Nanjing University. Prof. 
							Zhang's research interests include pattern 
							recognition, computer vision, and human-computer 
							interaction, as well as their applications in 
							digital image, digital video, and digital ink. Prof. 
							Zhang has published over 60 refereed journal and 
							conference paper.
Dr. Dmitry Ustalov
Research at Toloka AI, Russia
Dr. Dmitry Ustalov is the Head of Research at 
							Toloka, a global data labeling platform. He is 
							responsible for enabling the state-of-the-art 
							methods for quality control in Toloka and spreading 
							the innovations made by the Toloka Research team. 
							His research interests focus on Crowdsourcing and 
							Natural Language Processing; his research is 
							published in the leading scientific venues, such as 
							NeurIPS, COLI, ACL, EACL, and EMNLP. Dmitry has been 
							co-organizing Crowd Science Workshop at NeurIPS and 
							VLDB since 2020, and TextGraphs workshop at ACL 
							since 2018. He co-authored and presented 
							crowdsourcing tutorials at NAACL-HLT '21, WWW '21, 
							SIGMOD/PODS '20, and WSDM '20.

NLPAI 2024---July 21-23, 2024 | 
							Chongqing, China (Online+Offline)
2024 5th International Conference on Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2024) which is co-sponsored by Sichuan 
							University and Chongqing University of Posts and 
							Telecommunications, hosted by Chongqing Jianzhu 
							College, supported by Xinjiang University, Tibet University and 
							Beijing Cas Spark Institute Information Technology was held successfully 
							during July 21-23, 2024 in Chongqing, China in the mode 
							of "online and offline".
More than 160 experts and 
							scholars from all over the world who participated in 
							the conference. The accepted and presented papers have 
							been published in Conference Proceedings. With the 
							joint efforts with the session chairs and 
							presenters, 8 oral sessions were held successfully. 
							Each session had selected one best presentation, and 
							it was awarded as the Best Presentation.
NLPAI 2023---August 4-6, 2023 | Urumqi, China (Online+Offline)
2023 4th International Conference on Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2023) which is sponsored by Xinjiang 
							University and Sichuan 
							University, supported by Tibet University and 
							Beijing Cas Spark Institute Information Technology was held successfully 
							during August 4-6, 2023 in Urumqi, China in the mode 
							of "online and offline".
More than 140 experts and 
							scholars from all over the world who participated in 
							the conference. The accepted and presented papers have 
							been published in Conference Proceedings. With the 
							joint efforts with the session chairs and 
							presenters, 10 oral sessions and 1 poster session were held successfully. 
							Each session had selected one best presentation, and 
							it was awarded as the Best Presentation.
NLPAI 2022---July 22-24, 2022---Chengdu, 
							China
2022 3rd International Conference on Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2022) which is sponsored by Sichuan 
							University, supported by Tibet University and 
							Xinjiang University was held successfully during 
							July 22-24, 2022 with the mode of online.
More than 150 experts and 
							scholars from USA, Australia, Malaysia, Portugal, 
							Singapore, Republic of Korea, UK, India, Thailand, 
							China, Germany, Japan, Canada, and so on attended 
							the conference. The accepted and presented papers have 
							been published in Conference Proceedings. With the 
							joint efforts with the session chairs and 
							presenters, 8 oral sessions were held successfully. 
							Each session had selected one best presentation, and 
							it was awarded as the Best Presentation.
NLPAI 2021---July 16-18, 2021---Chengdu, 
							China
2021 2nd International Conference on Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2021) which is sponsored by Sichuan 
							University, supported by Tibet University and 
							Xinjiang University was held successfully during 
							July 16-18, 2021 with the mode of online and 
							offline.
There were about 120 delegates 
							from all over the world who participated in the 
							conference. The accepted and presented papers have 
							been published in Conference Proceedings. With the 
							joint efforts with the session chairs and 
							presenters, 7 oral sessions were held successfully. 
							Each session had selected one best presentation, and 
							it was awarded as the Best Presentation. The details 
							can be found
here
.
NLPAI 2020---July 16-18, 2020---Chengdu, 
							China
2020 International Conference on 
							Natural Language Processing and Artificial 
							Intelligence (NLPAI 2020) which is planned to be 
							held in Chengdu, China was held successfully during 
							July 16-18, 2020 with the mode of online. Experts 
							and scholars from all over the world participated 
							and presented at the conference. The conference 
							papers were published in Conference Proceedings, 
							indexed by Ei Compendex and Scopus.

							Xinjiang, under Double First Class University Plan 
							and former Project 211. Xinjiang University 
							comprises fourteen schools, which has courses and

							law and economics to management, the arts and 
							history.

VISA TO CHINA
Conference participants may need 
							a Chinese visa to enter China. Specifically, those 
							who are not Chinese citizens, except passport 
							holders from Singapore, Brunei, and Japan, need a 
							Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a visa at 
							the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region in 
							which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
							will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
							possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
							Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a 
							Tourist ("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT 
							required for obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary Materials to Apply for Chinese 
							Visa
脗禄A passport which is valid for at 
							least six months following the date of submission, 
							with an entire blank page available for the visa;
脗禄One Visa Application Form of the Peoples Republic 
							of China, which is filled clearly with true, 
							authentic and complete information and signed by the 
							applicant;
脗禄One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded 
							passport photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or 
							digital photo printed on the ordinary paper shall 
							not be accepted);
脗禄Other related application materials according to 
							different purposes for your trip to China;
脗禄Other materials related to the visa application, 
							deemed as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local 
							Chinese Embassy in your country or visit the 
							website:
http://www.visaforchina.org/
.
Alternatively, participants may ask their travel 
							agents to arrange their travel to China and obtain a 
							tourist visa irrespective of whether or not they 
							have registered with the Conference Secretariat. If 
							participants have any difficulties in obtaining 
							their visa, they should contact the Conference 
							Secretary.
Invitation Letter
Please note NLPAI 2026 is not 
							authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond 
							providing the Notification of Acceptance Letter and 
							Invitation Letter issued by the organizing 
							committee.
* Invitation letter will only be issued upon request 
							after successful registration.
Note:
Should your application be denied, NLPAI cannot 
							change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign 
							Affairs, nor will NLPAI engage in discussion or 
							correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on 
							behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot 
							be refunded when the VISA application of individual 
							being denied.

July 10-13, 2026, 
								Urumqi, China
Previous
Next
INTRODUCTION
Welcome to the official website of 
							2026 7th International Conference on Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2026)! 
							NLPAI 2026 will be held in Urumqi, China during 
							July 10-13, 2026. NLPAI 2026 aims 
							to provide a forum for researchers, practitioners, 
							and professionals from the industry, academia and 
							government to discourse on research and development, 
							professionals practice in the field of Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence.
We welcome 
							researchers, engineers, students, industry, 
							investors and people from all walks of life 
							interested in Natural Language Processing and 
							Artificial Intelligence to attend the conference and 
							share your experiences and lessons with other 
							enthusiasts, and develop opportunities for 
							cooperation
.
PAPER PUBLICATION
Submitted papers will be peer reviewed by Technical 
							Committee members or international reviewers. All 
							accepted papers will be published in
International 
							Conference Proceedings by ACM
(ISBN:
							979-8-4007-2497-8)
, which will be archived in
ACM Digital 
							Library
, indexed by
Ei Compendex
and
Scopus
, and 
							submitted to be reviewed by Thomson Reuters 
							Conference Proceedings Citation Index (ISI Web of 
							Science).
To be published in the Conference 
							Proceedings, the author of an accepted paper is 
							required to register for the conference and present 
							his/her paper.
NEWS
All accepted papers will be published in International Conference Proceedings by ACM(ISBN: 979-8-4007-2497-8)

PAPER 
			SUBMISSIONS
Author Instructions
The official conference language 
							is English. The submissions should contain original, 
							high quality, not submitted or published elsewhere 
							work. Papers should be submitted electronically in 
							pdf format and should be within 10 pages in single 
							column/5 pages in double columns including all 
							figures, tables, and references.
If the paper is 
							more than 10 pages in single column/5 pages in 
							double columns, additional pages will be charged 
							(60USD/420CNY/per page). Detailed paper templates 
							may be found here.
Conference 
							Proceedings Template
For those who want to do presentation only without 
							paper publication, the abstract template maybe found 
							here:
Abstract 
							Template

:

							Notification of Acceptance Letter and Review Form 
							will be sent from the conference email box on the 
							due date if the paper is accepted.

Best Oral/Poster Presentation

: One Best Oral/Poster Presentation will be

VISA TO CHINA
Conference participants may need 
							a Chinese visa to enter China. Specifically, those 
							who are not Chinese citizens, except passport 
							holders from Singapore, Brunei, and Japan, need a 
							Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a visa at 
							the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region in 
							which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
							will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
							possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
							Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a 
							Tourist ("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT 
							required for obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary Materials to Apply for Chinese 
							Visa
脗禄A passport which is valid for at 
							least six months following the date of submission, 
							with an entire blank page available for the visa;
脗禄One Visa Application Form of the Peoples Republic 
							of China, which is filled clearly with true, 
							authentic and complete information and signed by the 
							applicant;
脗禄One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded 
							passport photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or 
							digital photo printed on the ordinary paper shall 
							not be accepted);
脗禄Other related application materials according to 
							different purposes for your trip to China;
脗禄Other materials related to the visa application, 
							deemed as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local 
							Chinese Embassy in your country or visit the 
							website:
http://www.visaforchina.org/
.
Alternatively, participants may ask their travel 
							agents to arrange their travel to China and obtain a 
							tourist visa irrespective of whether or not they 
							have registered with the Conference Secretariat. If 
							participants have any difficulties in obtaining 
							their visa, they should contact the Conference 
							Secretary.
Invitation Letter
Please note NLPAI 2026 is not 
							authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond 
							providing the Notification of Acceptance Letter and 
							Invitation Letter issued by the organizing 
							committee.
* Invitation letter will only be issued upon request 
							after successful registration.
Note:
Should your application be denied, NLPAI cannot 
							change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign 
							Affairs, nor will NLPAI engage in discussion or 
							correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on 
							behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot 
							be refunded when the VISA application of individual 
							being denied.

Link: KEYNOTE SPEAKERS
Title: NLPAI 2026|Urumiqi,China
NLPAI 2026|Urumiqi,China
Skip to content
2026 7th International 
								Conference on
Natural Language Processing 
								and Artificial Intelligence (NLPAI 2026)
July 10-13, 2026, 
								Urumqi, China
KEYNOTE 
			SPEAKER
of NLPAI 2025
Prof.
Yue Zhang
Westlake University
, China
Yue Zhang is a tenured Professor 
							at Westlake University. His research interests 
							include NLP and its underlying machine learning 
							algorithms. His major contributions to the field 
							include psycholinguistically motivated machine 
							learning algorithm, learning-guided beam search for 
							structured prediction, pioneering neural NLP models 
							including graph LSTM, and OOD generalization for 
							NLP. He authored the Cambridge University Press book 
							``Natural Language Processing -- a Machine Learning 
							Perspective''. He is the PC co-chair for CCL 2020 
							and EMNLP 2022, and action editor for Transactios 
							for ACL. He also served as associate editor for 
							IEEE/ACM Transactions of Audio Speech and Language 
							Processing (TASLP), ACM Transactions on Asian and 
							Low-Resource Languages (TALLIP), IEEE Transactions 
							on Big Data (TBD) and Computer, Speech and Language 
							(CSL). He won the best paper awards of IALP 2017 and 
							COLING 2018, best paper honorable mention of SemEval 
							2020, and best paper nomination for ACL 2018 and ACL 
							2023.
Speech Title: "AI Scientist"
Abstract: The 
							rise of LLMs allows systems to perform complex 
							reasoning, which leads to the exploration of AI 
							scientists. Ideally, AI scientists should be able to 
							generate new ideas, rea relevant literature, design 
							the implementation, conduct experiments, and write 
							academic reports. Existing research has achieved 
							initial milestones on some of the functionalities. 
							In this talk, I will share recent work on using AI 
							to facilitate research activities, including 
							literature survey, recommendation, and knowledge 
							organization. Then I will discuss a system that 
							automatically conducts research given a direction, 
							working towards a finished article, and revising its 
							work according to reviewer comments.

Link: INVITED SPEAKERS
Title: NLPAI 2026|Urumiqi,China
NLPAI 2026|Urumiqi,China
Skip to content
2026 7th International 
								Conference on
Natural Language Processing 
								and Artificial Intelligence (NLPAI 2026)
July 10-13, 2026, 
								Urumqi, China
INVITED 
			SPEAKER
S
Assoc. Prof. Samir Rustamov
ADA University, Azerbaijan
I got my Ph.D. degree in Computer Science from 
							Institute of Cybernetics and I was post-doctoral 
							researcher at Georgia Institute of Technology, USA. 
							I am an Associate Professor in School of IT and 
							Engineering, ADA University. My interests are 
							natural language processing and machine learning. I 
							am particularly interested in how to use language 
							understanding to generalize and learn more 
							efficiently. I am also head of AI laboratory at 
							MegaSec Company. I have worked on a range of 
							applications including Speech Recogition, Speech 
							Synthesis, Dialogue System, Deep Learning, Sentiment 
							Analysis and developed AI -based products for 
							different companies. I help organisations to build 
							AI and machine learning into their products and 
							services, with a particular focus on voice and 
							language technology.
Speech Title: "From Lab to 
							Reality: Building Practical Speech Recognition 
							Systems"
PREVIOUS
SPEAKER
S
Prof. Xiwen Zhang
Beijing Language and Culture University, China
Xiwen Zhang is currently a Professor of Digital 
							Media Department, School of Information Science in 
							the Beijing Language and Culture University. He 
							worked as an associated professor from 2002 to 2007 
							at the Human-computer interaction Laboratory, 
							Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences. 
							From 2005 to 2006 he was a Post doctor advised by 
							Prof. Michael R. Lyu in the Department of Computer 
							Science and Engineering, the Chinese University of 
							Hong Kong. From February to April in 2001 he was a 
							Research Assistant by Dr. KeZhang Chen in the 
							Department of Mechanical Engineering, the University 
							of Hong Kong. From 2000 to 2002 he was a Post doctor 
							advised by Prof. ShiJie Cai in the Computer Science 
							and Technology department, Nanjing University. Prof. 
							Zhang's research interests include pattern 
							recognition, computer vision, and human-computer 
							interaction, as well as their applications in 
							digital image, digital video, and digital ink. Prof. 
							Zhang has published over 60 refereed journal and 
							conference paper.
Dr. Dmitry Ustalov
Research at Toloka AI, Russia
Dr. Dmitry Ustalov is the Head of Research at 
							Toloka, a global data labeling platform. He is 
							responsible for enabling the state-of-the-art 
							methods for quality control in Toloka and spreading 
							the innovations made by the Toloka Research team. 
							His research interests focus on Crowdsourcing and 
							Natural Language Processing; his research is 
							published in the leading scientific venues, such as 
							NeurIPS, COLI, ACL, EACL, and EMNLP. Dmitry has been 
							co-organizing Crowd Science Workshop at NeurIPS and 
							VLDB since 2020, and TextGraphs workshop at ACL 
							since 2018. He co-authored and presented 
							crowdsourcing tutorials at NAACL-HLT '21, WWW '21, 
							SIGMOD/PODS '20, and WSDM '20.

Xinjiang University (XJU) is one 
							of the major universities in the Xinjiang Uyghur 
							Autonomous Region, People's Republic of China and is 
							a national key university. Established in 1924 in 
							Urumqi, Xinjiang University is a comprehensive 
							university with the highest academic level in 
							Xinjiang, under Double First Class University Plan

KEYNOTE 
			SPEAKER
of NLPAI 2025
Prof.
Yue Zhang
Westlake University
, China
Yue Zhang is a tenured Professor 
							at Westlake University. His research interests 
							include NLP and its underlying machine learning 
							algorithms. His major contributions to the field 
							include psycholinguistically motivated machine 
							learning algorithm, learning-guided beam search for 
							structured prediction, pioneering neural NLP models 
							including graph LSTM, and OOD generalization for 
							NLP. He authored the Cambridge University Press book 
							``Natural Language Processing -- a Machine Learning 
							Perspective''. He is the PC co-chair for CCL 2020 
							and EMNLP 2022, and action editor for Transactios 
							for ACL. He also served as associate editor for 
							IEEE/ACM Transactions of Audio Speech and Language 
							Processing (TASLP), ACM Transactions on Asian and 
							Low-Resource Languages (TALLIP), IEEE Transactions 
							on Big Data (TBD) and Computer, Speech and Language 
							(CSL). He won the best paper awards of IALP 2017 and 
							COLING 2018, best paper honorable mention of SemEval 
							2020, and best paper nomination for ACL 2018 and ACL 
							2023.
Speech Title: "AI Scientist"
Abstract: The 
							rise of LLMs allows systems to perform complex 
							reasoning, which leads to the exploration of AI 
							scientists. Ideally, AI scientists should be able to 
							generate new ideas, rea relevant literature, design 
							the implementation, conduct experiments, and write 
							academic reports. Existing research has achieved 
							initial milestones on some of the functionalities. 
							In this talk, I will share recent work on using AI 
							to facilitate research activities, including 
							literature survey, recommendation, and knowledge 
							organization. Then I will discuss a system that 
							automatically conducts research given a direction, 
							working towards a finished article, and revising its 
							work according to reviewer comments.

VISA TO CHINA
Conference participants may need 
							a Chinese visa to enter China. Specifically, those 
							who are not Chinese citizens, except passport 
							holders from Singapore, Brunei, and Japan, need a 
							Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a visa at 
							the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region in 
							which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
							will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
							possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
							Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a 
							Tourist ("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT 
							required for obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary Materials to Apply for Chinese 
							Visa
脗禄A passport which is valid for at 
							least six months following the date of submission, 
							with an entire blank page available for the visa;
脗禄One Visa Application Form of the Peoples Republic 
							of China, which is filled clearly with true, 
							authentic and complete information and signed by the 
							applicant;
脗禄One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded 
							passport photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or 
							digital photo printed on the ordinary paper shall 
							not be accepted);
脗禄Other related application materials according to 
							different purposes for your trip to China;
脗禄Other materials related to the visa application, 
							deemed as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local 
							Chinese Embassy in your country or visit the 
							website:
http://www.visaforchina.org/
.
Alternatively, participants may ask their travel 
							agents to arrange their travel to China and obtain a 
							tourist visa irrespective of whether or not they 
							have registered with the Conference Secretariat. If 
							participants have any difficulties in obtaining 
							their visa, they should contact the Conference 
							Secretary.
Invitation Letter
Please note NLPAI 2026 is not 
							authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond 
							providing the Notification of Acceptance Letter and 
							Invitation Letter issued by the organizing 
							committee.
* Invitation letter will only be issued upon request 
							after successful registration.
Note:
Should your application be denied, NLPAI cannot 
							change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign 
							Affairs, nor will NLPAI engage in discussion or 
							correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on 
							behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot 
							be refunded when the VISA application of individual 
							being denied.

Previous

INTRODUCTION
Welcome to the official website of 
							2026 7th International Conference on Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2026)! 
							NLPAI 2026 will be held in Urumqi, China during 
							July 10-13, 2026. NLPAI 2026 aims 
							to provide a forum for researchers, practitioners, 
							and professionals from the industry, academia and 
							government to discourse on research and development, 
							professionals practice in the field of Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence.
We welcome 
							researchers, engineers, students, industry, 
							investors and people from all walks of life 
							interested in Natural Language Processing and 
							Artificial Intelligence to attend the conference and 
							share your experiences and lessons with other 
							enthusiasts, and develop opportunities for 
							cooperation
.
PAPER PUBLICATION
Submitted papers will be peer reviewed by Technical 
							Committee members or international reviewers. All 
							accepted papers will be published in
International 
							Conference Proceedings by ACM
(ISBN:
							979-8-4007-2497-8)
, which will be archived in
ACM Digital 
							Library
, indexed by
Ei Compendex
and
Scopus
, and 
							submitted to be reviewed by Thomson Reuters 
							Conference Proceedings Citation Index (ISI Web of 
							Science).
To be published in the Conference 
							Proceedings, the author of an accepted paper is 
							required to register for the conference and present 
							his/her paper.
NEWS
All accepted papers will be published in International Conference Proceedings by ACM(ISBN: 979-8-4007-2497-8)

Title: NLPAI 2026|Urumiqi,China
NLPAI 2026|Urumiqi,China

2026 7th International

Natural Language Processing 
								and Artificial Intelligence (NLPAI 2026)
July 10-13, 2026, 
								Urumqi, China
KEYNOTE 
			SPEAKER
of NLPAI 2025
Prof.
Yue Zhang
Westlake University
, China
Yue Zhang is a tenured Professor 
							at Westlake University. His research interests 
							include NLP and its underlying machine learning 
							algorithms. His major contributions to the field 
							include psycholinguistically motivated machine 
							learning algorithm, learning-guided beam search for 
							structured prediction, pioneering neural NLP models 
							including graph LSTM, and OOD generalization for 
							NLP. He authored the Cambridge University Press book 
							``Natural Language Processing -- a Machine Learning 
							Perspective''. He is the PC co-chair for CCL 2020 
							and EMNLP 2022, and action editor for Transactios 
							for ACL. He also served as associate editor for 
							IEEE/ACM Transactions of Audio Speech and Language 
							Processing (TASLP), ACM Transactions on Asian and 
							Low-Resource Languages (TALLIP), IEEE Transactions 
							on Big Data (TBD) and Computer, Speech and Language 
							(CSL). He won the best paper awards of IALP 2017 and 
							COLING 2018, best paper honorable mention of SemEval 
							2020, and best paper nomination for ACL 2018 and ACL 
							2023.
Speech Title: "AI Scientist"
Abstract: The 
							rise of LLMs allows systems to perform complex 
							reasoning, which leads to the exploration of AI 
							scientists. Ideally, AI scientists should be able to 
							generate new ideas, rea relevant literature, design 
							the implementation, conduct experiments, and write 
							academic reports. Existing research has achieved 
							initial milestones on some of the functionalities. 
							In this talk, I will share recent work on using AI 
							to facilitate research activities, including 
							literature survey, recommendation, and knowledge 
							organization. Then I will discuss a system that 
							automatically conducts research given a direction, 
							working towards a finished article, and revising its 
							work according to reviewer comments.

I got my Ph.D. degree in Computer Science from

							am particularly interested in how to use language 
							understanding to generalize and learn more

							Synthesis, Dialogue System, Deep Learning, Sentiment

Speech Title: "From Lab to

PREVIOUS
SPEAKER
S
Prof. Xiwen Zhang
Beijing Language and Culture University, China
Xiwen Zhang is currently a Professor of Digital 
							Media Department, School of Information Science in 
							the Beijing Language and Culture University. He 
							worked as an associated professor from 2002 to 2007 
							at the Human-computer interaction Laboratory, 
							Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences. 
							From 2005 to 2006 he was a Post doctor advised by 
							Prof. Michael R. Lyu in the Department of Computer 
							Science and Engineering, the Chinese University of 
							Hong Kong. From February to April in 2001 he was a 
							Research Assistant by Dr. KeZhang Chen in the 
							Department of Mechanical Engineering, the University 
							of Hong Kong. From 2000 to 2002 he was a Post doctor 
							advised by Prof. ShiJie Cai in the Computer Science 
							and Technology department, Nanjing University. Prof. 
							Zhang's research interests include pattern 
							recognition, computer vision, and human-computer 
							interaction, as well as their applications in 
							digital image, digital video, and digital ink. Prof. 
							Zhang has published over 60 refereed journal and 
							conference paper.
Dr. Dmitry Ustalov
Research at Toloka AI, Russia
Dr. Dmitry Ustalov is the Head of Research at 
							Toloka, a global data labeling platform. He is 
							responsible for enabling the state-of-the-art 
							methods for quality control in Toloka and spreading 
							the innovations made by the Toloka Research team. 
							His research interests focus on Crowdsourcing and 
							Natural Language Processing; his research is 
							published in the leading scientific venues, such as 
							NeurIPS, COLI, ACL, EACL, and EMNLP. Dmitry has been 
							co-organizing Crowd Science Workshop at NeurIPS and 
							VLDB since 2020, and TextGraphs workshop at ACL 
							since 2018. He co-authored and presented 
							crowdsourcing tutorials at NAACL-HLT '21, WWW '21, 
							SIGMOD/PODS '20, and WSDM '20.

CALL FOR SPONSORS
2026 7th International Conference 
							on Natural Language Processing and Artificial 
							Intelligence (NLPAI 2026) is the premier forum for 
							the presentation of new advances and research 
							results in the fields of theoretical, experimental, 
							and applied Natural Language Processing and 
							Artificial Intelligence. The conference will bring 
							together leading researchers, engineers and 
							scientists in the domain of interest from around the 
							world.
Support of the NLPAI gives your organization the 
							opportunity to meet and engage with the top 
							researchers and professionals in the area of Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence. In 
							addition to the leading authorities in the field in 
							academia and the business sector, the conference 
							also attracts postdoctoral researchers, as well as 
							doctoral and Master芒聙聶s students seeking engagement 
							with industry.

							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence

							University and Sichuan 
							University, supported by Tibet University and 
							Beijing Cas Spark Institute Information Technology was held successfully 
							during August 4-6, 2023 in Urumqi, China in the mode 
							of "online and offline".
More than 140 experts and 
							scholars from all over the world who participated in 
							the conference. The accepted and presented papers have

Natural Language Processing

July 10-13, 2026,

VISA TO CHINA
Conference participants may need 
							a Chinese visa to enter China. Specifically, those 
							who are not Chinese citizens, except passport 
							holders from Singapore, Brunei, and Japan, need a 
							Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a visa at 
							the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region in 
							which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
							will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
							possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
							Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a 
							Tourist ("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT 
							required for obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary Materials to Apply for Chinese 
							Visa
脗禄A passport which is valid for at 
							least six months following the date of submission, 
							with an entire blank page available for the visa;
脗禄One Visa Application Form of the Peoples Republic 
							of China, which is filled clearly with true, 
							authentic and complete information and signed by the 
							applicant;
脗禄One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded 
							passport photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or 
							digital photo printed on the ordinary paper shall 
							not be accepted);
脗禄Other related application materials according to 
							different purposes for your trip to China;
脗禄Other materials related to the visa application, 
							deemed as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local 
							Chinese Embassy in your country or visit the 
							website:
http://www.visaforchina.org/
.
Alternatively, participants may ask their travel 
							agents to arrange their travel to China and obtain a 
							tourist visa irrespective of whether or not they 
							have registered with the Conference Secretariat. If 
							participants have any difficulties in obtaining 
							their visa, they should contact the Conference 
							Secretary.
Invitation Letter
Please note NLPAI 2026 is not 
							authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond 
							providing the Notification of Acceptance Letter and 
							Invitation Letter issued by the organizing 
							committee.
* Invitation letter will only be issued upon request 
							after successful registration.
Note:
Should your application be denied, NLPAI cannot 
							change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign 
							Affairs, nor will NLPAI engage in discussion or 
							correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on 
							behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot 
							be refunded when the VISA application of individual 
							being denied.

2026 7th International

Natural Language Processing 
								and Artificial Intelligence (NLPAI 2026)
July 10-13, 2026, 
								Urumqi, China
Previous
Next
INTRODUCTION
Welcome to the official website of 
							2026 7th International Conference on Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2026)! 
							NLPAI 2026 will be held in Urumqi, China during 
							July 10-13, 2026. NLPAI 2026 aims 
							to provide a forum for researchers, practitioners, 
							and professionals from the industry, academia and 
							government to discourse on research and development, 
							professionals practice in the field of Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence.
We welcome 
							researchers, engineers, students, industry, 
							investors and people from all walks of life 
							interested in Natural Language Processing and 
							Artificial Intelligence to attend the conference and 
							share your experiences and lessons with other 
							enthusiasts, and develop opportunities for 
							cooperation
.
PAPER PUBLICATION
Submitted papers will be peer reviewed by Technical 
							Committee members or international reviewers. All 
							accepted papers will be published in
International 
							Conference Proceedings by ACM
(ISBN:
							979-8-4007-2497-8)
, which will be archived in
ACM Digital 
							Library
, indexed by
Ei Compendex
and
Scopus
, and 
							submitted to be reviewed by Thomson Reuters 
							Conference Proceedings Citation Index (ISI Web of 
							Science).
To be published in the Conference 
							Proceedings, the author of an accepted paper is 
							required to register for the conference and present 
							his/her paper.
NEWS
All accepted papers will be published in International Conference Proceedings by ACM(ISBN: 979-8-4007-2497-8)

KEYNOTE 
			SPEAKER
of NLPAI 2025
Prof.
Yue Zhang
Westlake University
, China
Yue Zhang is a tenured Professor 
							at Westlake University. His research interests 
							include NLP and its underlying machine learning 
							algorithms. His major contributions to the field 
							include psycholinguistically motivated machine 
							learning algorithm, learning-guided beam search for 
							structured prediction, pioneering neural NLP models 
							including graph LSTM, and OOD generalization for 
							NLP. He authored the Cambridge University Press book 
							``Natural Language Processing -- a Machine Learning 
							Perspective''. He is the PC co-chair for CCL 2020 
							and EMNLP 2022, and action editor for Transactios 
							for ACL. He also served as associate editor for 
							IEEE/ACM Transactions of Audio Speech and Language 
							Processing (TASLP), ACM Transactions on Asian and 
							Low-Resource Languages (TALLIP), IEEE Transactions 
							on Big Data (TBD) and Computer, Speech and Language 
							(CSL). He won the best paper awards of IALP 2017 and 
							COLING 2018, best paper honorable mention of SemEval 
							2020, and best paper nomination for ACL 2018 and ACL 
							2023.
Speech Title: "AI Scientist"
Abstract: The 
							rise of LLMs allows systems to perform complex 
							reasoning, which leads to the exploration of AI 
							scientists. Ideally, AI scientists should be able to 
							generate new ideas, rea relevant literature, design 
							the implementation, conduct experiments, and write 
							academic reports. Existing research has achieved 
							initial milestones on some of the functionalities. 
							In this talk, I will share recent work on using AI 
							to facilitate research activities, including 
							literature survey, recommendation, and knowledge 
							organization. Then I will discuss a system that 
							automatically conducts research given a direction, 
							working towards a finished article, and revising its 
							work according to reviewer comments.

I got my Ph.D. degree in Computer Science from 
							Institute of Cybernetics and I was post-doctoral 
							researcher at Georgia Institute of Technology, USA. 
							I am an Associate Professor in School of IT and 
							Engineering, ADA University. My interests are 
							natural language processing and machine learning. I 
							am particularly interested in how to use language 
							understanding to generalize and learn more 
							efficiently. I am also head of AI laboratory at 
							MegaSec Company. I have worked on a range of 
							applications including Speech Recogition, Speech 
							Synthesis, Dialogue System, Deep Learning, Sentiment 
							Analysis and developed AI -based products for 
							different companies. I help organisations to build 
							AI and machine learning into their products and 
							services, with a particular focus on voice and 
							language technology.
Speech Title: "From Lab to 
							Reality: Building Practical Speech Recognition 
							Systems"
PREVIOUS
SPEAKER
S
Prof. Xiwen Zhang
Beijing Language and Culture University, China
Xiwen Zhang is currently a Professor of Digital 
							Media Department, School of Information Science in 
							the Beijing Language and Culture University. He 
							worked as an associated professor from 2002 to 2007 
							at the Human-computer interaction Laboratory, 
							Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences. 
							From 2005 to 2006 he was a Post doctor advised by 
							Prof. Michael R. Lyu in the Department of Computer 
							Science and Engineering, the Chinese University of 
							Hong Kong. From February to April in 2001 he was a 
							Research Assistant by Dr. KeZhang Chen in the 
							Department of Mechanical Engineering, the University 
							of Hong Kong. From 2000 to 2002 he was a Post doctor 
							advised by Prof. ShiJie Cai in the Computer Science 
							and Technology department, Nanjing University. Prof. 
							Zhang's research interests include pattern 
							recognition, computer vision, and human-computer 
							interaction, as well as their applications in 
							digital image, digital video, and digital ink. Prof. 
							Zhang has published over 60 refereed journal and 
							conference paper.
Dr. Dmitry Ustalov
Research at Toloka AI, Russia
Dr. Dmitry Ustalov is the Head of Research at 
							Toloka, a global data labeling platform. He is 
							responsible for enabling the state-of-the-art 
							methods for quality control in Toloka and spreading 
							the innovations made by the Toloka Research team. 
							His research interests focus on Crowdsourcing and 
							Natural Language Processing; his research is 
							published in the leading scientific venues, such as 
							NeurIPS, COLI, ACL, EACL, and EMNLP. Dmitry has been 
							co-organizing Crowd Science Workshop at NeurIPS and 
							VLDB since 2020, and TextGraphs workshop at ACL 
							since 2018. He co-authored and presented 
							crowdsourcing tutorials at NAACL-HLT '21, WWW '21, 
							SIGMOD/PODS '20, and WSDM '20.

Xinjiang University (XJU) is one 
							of the major universities in the Xinjiang Uyghur 
							Autonomous Region, People's Republic of China and is 
							a national key university. Established in 1924 in 
							Urumqi, Xinjiang University is a comprehensive 
							university with the highest academic level in 
							Xinjiang, under Double First Class University Plan 
							and former Project 211. Xinjiang University 
							comprises fourteen schools, which has courses and 
							branches of study ranging from science, engineering, 
							law and economics to management, the arts and 
							history.

VISA TO CHINA
Conference participants may need 
							a Chinese visa to enter China. Specifically, those 
							who are not Chinese citizens, except passport 
							holders from Singapore, Brunei, and Japan, need a 
							Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a visa at 
							the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region in 
							which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
							will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
							possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
							Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a 
							Tourist ("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT 
							required for obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary Materials to Apply for Chinese 
							Visa
脗禄A passport which is valid for at 
							least six months following the date of submission, 
							with an entire blank page available for the visa;
脗禄One Visa Application Form of the Peoples Republic 
							of China, which is filled clearly with true, 
							authentic and complete information and signed by the 
							applicant;
脗禄One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded 
							passport photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or 
							digital photo printed on the ordinary paper shall 
							not be accepted);
脗禄Other related application materials according to 
							different purposes for your trip to China;
脗禄Other materials related to the visa application, 
							deemed as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local 
							Chinese Embassy in your country or visit the 
							website:
http://www.visaforchina.org/
.
Alternatively, participants may ask their travel 
							agents to arrange their travel to China and obtain a 
							tourist visa irrespective of whether or not they 
							have registered with the Conference Secretariat. If 
							participants have any difficulties in obtaining 
							their visa, they should contact the Conference 
							Secretary.
Invitation Letter
Please note NLPAI 2026 is not 
							authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond 
							providing the Notification of Acceptance Letter and 
							Invitation Letter issued by the organizing 
							committee.
* Invitation letter will only be issued upon request 
							after successful registration.
Note:
Should your application be denied, NLPAI cannot 
							change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign 
							Affairs, nor will NLPAI engage in discussion or 
							correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on 
							behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot 
							be refunded when the VISA application of individual 
							being denied.

Previous

INTRODUCTION
Welcome to the official website of 
							2026 7th International Conference on Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2026)! 
							NLPAI 2026 will be held in Urumqi, China during 
							July 10-13, 2026. NLPAI 2026 aims 
							to provide a forum for researchers, practitioners, 
							and professionals from the industry, academia and 
							government to discourse on research and development, 
							professionals practice in the field of Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence.
We welcome 
							researchers, engineers, students, industry, 
							investors and people from all walks of life 
							interested in Natural Language Processing and 
							Artificial Intelligence to attend the conference and 
							share your experiences and lessons with other 
							enthusiasts, and develop opportunities for 
							cooperation
.
PAPER PUBLICATION
Submitted papers will be peer reviewed by Technical 
							Committee members or international reviewers. All 
							accepted papers will be published in
International 
							Conference Proceedings by ACM
(ISBN:
							979-8-4007-2497-8)
, which will be archived in
ACM Digital 
							Library
, indexed by
Ei Compendex
and
Scopus
, and 
							submitted to be reviewed by Thomson Reuters 
							Conference Proceedings Citation Index (ISI Web of 
							Science).
To be published in the Conference 
							Proceedings, the author of an accepted paper is 
							required to register for the conference and present 
							his/her paper.
NEWS
All accepted papers will be published in International Conference Proceedings by ACM(ISBN: 979-8-4007-2497-8)

									highlight a series of Special Sessions to 
									complement the regular program with new or 
									emerging topics of particular interest to 
									the Natural Language Processing and 
									Artificial Intelligence. Prospective 
									organizers of Special Sessions should submit 
									proposals with the following information:Title of Special SessionBrief 
									description of Special SessionRelated 
									topicsName, Affiliation, E-mail, Short 
									biography of the organizersList of five 
									to six contributed papers (including titles, 
									authors, contact information of the 
									corresponding author, and a short abstract). 
									Upon acceptance of the special session 
									proposal, the contributed papers will be 
									submitted in the same format as regular 
									papers. Organizers should not contribute 
									more than one paper. |

			SUBMISSIONS
Author Instructions
The official conference language 
							is English. The submissions should contain original, 
							high quality, not submitted or published elsewhere 
							work. Papers should be submitted electronically in 
							pdf format and should be within 10 pages in single 
							column/5 pages in double columns including all 
							figures, tables, and references.
If the paper is 
							more than 10 pages in single column/5 pages in 
							double columns, additional pages will be charged 
							(60USD/420CNY/per page). Detailed paper templates 
							may be found here.

							algorithms. His major contributions to the field 
							include psycholinguistically motivated machine 
							learning algorithm, learning-guided beam search for 
							structured prediction, pioneering neural NLP models 
							including graph LSTM, and OOD generalization for 
							NLP. He authored the Cambridge University Press book 
							``Natural Language Processing -- a Machine Learning 
							Perspective''. He is the PC co-chair for CCL 2020 
							and EMNLP 2022, and action editor for Transactios 
							for ACL. He also served as associate editor for 
							IEEE/ACM Transactions of Audio Speech and Language 
							Processing (TASLP), ACM Transactions on Asian and 
							Low-Resource Languages (TALLIP), IEEE Transactions 
							on Big Data (TBD) and Computer, Speech and Language 
							(CSL). He won the best paper awards of IALP 2017 and 
							COLING 2018, best paper honorable mention of SemEval 
							2020, and best paper nomination for ACL 2018 and ACL 
							2023.
Speech Title: "AI Scientist"
Abstract: The 
							rise of LLMs allows systems to perform complex 
							reasoning, which leads to the exploration of AI 
							scientists. Ideally, AI scientists should be able to 
							generate new ideas, rea relevant literature, design 
							the implementation, conduct experiments, and write 
							academic reports. Existing research has achieved 
							initial milestones on some of the functionalities. 
							In this talk, I will share recent work on using AI 
							to facilitate research activities, including 
							literature survey, recommendation, and knowledge 
							organization. Then I will discuss a system that 
							automatically conducts research given a direction, 
							working towards a finished article, and revising its 
							work according to reviewer comments.

VISA TO CHINA
Conference participants may need 
							a Chinese visa to enter China. Specifically, those 
							who are not Chinese citizens, except passport 
							holders from Singapore, Brunei, and Japan, need a 
							Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a visa at 
							the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region in 
							which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
							will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
							possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
							Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a 
							Tourist ("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT 
							required for obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary Materials to Apply for Chinese 
							Visa
脗禄A passport which is valid for at 
							least six months following the date of submission, 
							with an entire blank page available for the visa;
脗禄One Visa Application Form of the Peoples Republic 
							of China, which is filled clearly with true, 
							authentic and complete information and signed by the 
							applicant;
脗禄One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded 
							passport photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or 
							digital photo printed on the ordinary paper shall 
							not be accepted);
脗禄Other related application materials according to 
							different purposes for your trip to China;
脗禄Other materials related to the visa application, 
							deemed as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local 
							Chinese Embassy in your country or visit the 
							website:
http://www.visaforchina.org/
.
Alternatively, participants may ask their travel 
							agents to arrange their travel to China and obtain a 
							tourist visa irrespective of whether or not they 
							have registered with the Conference Secretariat. If 
							participants have any difficulties in obtaining 
							their visa, they should contact the Conference 
							Secretary.
Invitation Letter
Please note NLPAI 2026 is not 
							authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond 
							providing the Notification of Acceptance Letter and 
							Invitation Letter issued by the organizing 
							committee.
* Invitation letter will only be issued upon request 
							after successful registration.
Note:
Should your application be denied, NLPAI cannot 
							change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign 
							Affairs, nor will NLPAI engage in discussion or 
							correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on 
							behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot 
							be refunded when the VISA application of individual 
							being denied.

Previous

INTRODUCTION
Welcome to the official website of 
							2026 7th International Conference on Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence 
							(NLPAI 2026)! 
							NLPAI 2026 will be held in Urumqi, China during 
							July 10-13, 2026. NLPAI 2026 aims 
							to provide a forum for researchers, practitioners, 
							and professionals from the industry, academia and 
							government to discourse on research and development, 
							professionals practice in the field of Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence.
We welcome 
							researchers, engineers, students, industry, 
							investors and people from all walks of life 
							interested in Natural Language Processing and 
							Artificial Intelligence to attend the conference and 
							share your experiences and lessons with other 
							enthusiasts, and develop opportunities for 
							cooperation
.
PAPER PUBLICATION
Submitted papers will be peer reviewed by Technical 
							Committee members or international reviewers. All 
							accepted papers will be published in
International 
							Conference Proceedings by ACM
(ISBN:
							979-8-4007-2497-8)
, which will be archived in
ACM Digital 
							Library
, indexed by
Ei Compendex
and
Scopus
, and 
							submitted to be reviewed by Thomson Reuters 
							Conference Proceedings Citation Index (ISI Web of 
							Science).
To be published in the Conference 
							Proceedings, the author of an accepted paper is 
							required to register for the conference and present 
							his/her paper.
NEWS
All accepted papers will be published in International Conference Proceedings by ACM(ISBN: 979-8-4007-2497-8)

NLPAI 2026|Urumiqi,China

2026 7th International

Natural Language Processing

July 10-13, 2026,

KEYNOTE 
			SPEAKER
of NLPAI 2025
Prof.
Yue Zhang
Westlake University
, China
Yue Zhang is a tenured Professor 
							at Westlake University. His research interests 
							include NLP and its underlying machine learning 
							algorithms. His major contributions to the field 
							include psycholinguistically motivated machine 
							learning algorithm, learning-guided beam search for 
							structured prediction, pioneering neural NLP models 
							including graph LSTM, and OOD generalization for 
							NLP. He authored the Cambridge University Press book 
							``Natural Language Processing -- a Machine Learning 
							Perspective''. He is the PC co-chair for CCL 2020 
							and EMNLP 2022, and action editor for Transactios 
							for ACL. He also served as associate editor for 
							IEEE/ACM Transactions of Audio Speech and Language 
							Processing (TASLP), ACM Transactions on Asian and 
							Low-Resource Languages (TALLIP), IEEE Transactions 
							on Big Data (TBD) and Computer, Speech and Language 
							(CSL). He won the best paper awards of IALP 2017 and 
							COLING 2018, best paper honorable mention of SemEval 
							2020, and best paper nomination for ACL 2018 and ACL 
							2023.
Speech Title: "AI Scientist"
Abstract: The 
							rise of LLMs allows systems to perform complex 
							reasoning, which leads to the exploration of AI 
							scientists. Ideally, AI scientists should be able to 
							generate new ideas, rea relevant literature, design 
							the implementation, conduct experiments, and write 
							academic reports. Existing research has achieved 
							initial milestones on some of the functionalities. 
							In this talk, I will share recent work on using AI 
							to facilitate research activities, including 
							literature survey, recommendation, and knowledge 
							organization. Then I will discuss a system that 
							automatically conducts research given a direction, 
							working towards a finished article, and revising its 
							work according to reviewer comments.

S
Assoc. Prof. Samir Rustamov
ADA University, Azerbaijan
I got my Ph.D. degree in Computer Science from 
							Institute of Cybernetics and I was post-doctoral 
							researcher at Georgia Institute of Technology, USA. 
							I am an Associate Professor in School of IT and 
							Engineering, ADA University. My interests are 
							natural language processing and machine learning. I 
							am particularly interested in how to use language 
							understanding to generalize and learn more 
							efficiently. I am also head of AI laboratory at 
							MegaSec Company. I have worked on a range of 
							applications including Speech Recogition, Speech 
							Synthesis, Dialogue System, Deep Learning, Sentiment 
							Analysis and developed AI -based products for 
							different companies. I help organisations to build 
							AI and machine learning into their products and 
							services, with a particular focus on voice and 
							language technology.
Speech Title: "From Lab to 
							Reality: Building Practical Speech Recognition 
							Systems"
PREVIOUS
SPEAKER
S
Prof. Xiwen Zhang
Beijing Language and Culture University, China
Xiwen Zhang is currently a Professor of Digital 
							Media Department, School of Information Science in 
							the Beijing Language and Culture University. He 
							worked as an associated professor from 2002 to 2007 
							at the Human-computer interaction Laboratory, 
							Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences. 
							From 2005 to 2006 he was a Post doctor advised by 
							Prof. Michael R. Lyu in the Department of Computer 
							Science and Engineering, the Chinese University of 
							Hong Kong. From February to April in 2001 he was a 
							Research Assistant by Dr. KeZhang Chen in the 
							Department of Mechanical Engineering, the University 
							of Hong Kong. From 2000 to 2002 he was a Post doctor 
							advised by Prof. ShiJie Cai in the Computer Science 
							and Technology department, Nanjing University. Prof. 
							Zhang's research interests include pattern 
							recognition, computer vision, and human-computer 
							interaction, as well as their applications in 
							digital image, digital video, and digital ink. Prof. 
							Zhang has published over 60 refereed journal and 
							conference paper.
Dr. Dmitry Ustalov
Research at Toloka AI, Russia
Dr. Dmitry Ustalov is the Head of Research at 
							Toloka, a global data labeling platform. He is 
							responsible for enabling the state-of-the-art 
							methods for quality control in Toloka and spreading 
							the innovations made by the Toloka Research team. 
							His research interests focus on Crowdsourcing and 
							Natural Language Processing; his research is 
							published in the leading scientific venues, such as 
							NeurIPS, COLI, ACL, EACL, and EMNLP. Dmitry has been 
							co-organizing Crowd Science Workshop at NeurIPS and 
							VLDB since 2020, and TextGraphs workshop at ACL 
							since 2018. He co-authored and presented 
							crowdsourcing tutorials at NAACL-HLT '21, WWW '21, 
							SIGMOD/PODS '20, and WSDM '20.

2026 7th International Conference 
							on Natural Language Processing and Artificial 
							Intelligence (NLPAI 2026) is the premier forum for 
							the presentation of new advances and research 
							results in the fields of theoretical, experimental, 
							and applied Natural Language Processing and 
							Artificial Intelligence. The conference will bring 
							together leading researchers, engineers and 
							scientists in the domain of interest from around the 
							world.
Support of the NLPAI gives your organization the 
							opportunity to meet and engage with the top 
							researchers and professionals in the area of Natural 
							Language Processing and Artificial Intelligence. In 
							addition to the leading authorities in the field in 
							academia and the business sector, the conference 
							also attracts postdoctoral researchers, as well as 
							doctoral and Master芒聙聶s students seeking engagement

Natural Language Processing

VISA TO CHINA
Conference participants may need 
							a Chinese visa to enter China. Specifically, those 
							who are not Chinese citizens, except passport 
							holders from Singapore, Brunei, and Japan, need a 
							Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a visa at 
							the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region in 
							which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
							will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
							possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
							Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a 
							Tourist ("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT 
							required for obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary Materials to Apply for Chinese 
							Visa
脗禄A passport which is valid for at 
							least six months following the date of submission, 
							with an entire blank page available for the visa;
脗禄One Visa Application Form of the Peoples Republic 
							of China, which is filled clearly with true, 
							authentic and complete information and signed by the 
							applicant;
脗禄One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded 
							passport photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or 
							digital photo printed on the ordinary paper shall 
							not be accepted);
脗禄Other related application materials according to 
							different purposes for your trip to China;
脗禄Other materials related to the visa application, 
							deemed as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local 
							Chinese Embassy in your country or visit the 
							website:
http://www.visaforchina.org/
.
Alternatively, participants may ask their travel 
							agents to arrange their travel to China and obtain a 
							tourist visa irrespective of whether or not they 
							have registered with the Conference Secretariat. If 
							participants have any difficulties in obtaining 
							their visa, they should contact the Conference 
							Secretary.
Invitation Letter
Please note NLPAI 2026 is not 
							authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond 
							providing the Notification of Acceptance Letter and 
							Invitation Letter issued by the organizing 
							committee.
* Invitation letter will only be issued upon request 
							after successful registration.
Note:
Should your application be denied, NLPAI cannot 
							change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign 
							Affairs, nor will NLPAI engage in discussion or 
							correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on 
							behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot 
							be refunded when the VISA application of individual 
							being denied.
